name: Deva Hostel & Relax Pool
detail: Highly Recommended Hostel. Worth the price. Must stay.
area: Tha Phae,Chiang Mai - View on map

name: Siripanna Villa Resort & Spa Chiangmai
detail: I’ve stayed at 5 star resorts that are less than here.

name: Kokotel Chiang Mai Nimman
detail: 1. Comparing with the other hotels in Chiang Mai, Kokotel's price is very cheap. 2. The location is far away from the Old Town (4km which is not able to walk under the hot weather). You always need to take the tuk-tuk or songthaew to the Old Town. The hotel is near the Nimman District and you are able to take a walk about 10 minutes to the Nimman District. The location of the hotel is difficult to describe, most of the drivers did not realize the exact location. It would be better to ask the Road Number in Thailand Language in order to tell the tuk-tuk / songthaew drivers. 3. Room is clean and the toilet is also clean. 4. Staff is nice and helpful. They will provide the tourist information for you such as the Saturday/ Sunday night market. 5. Providing a breadfast starting from 07:00AM to 10:00AM. Helping us to call the airport taxi service at mid-night. Although the taxi service is not provided by the hotel, the taxi driver is also polite and helping us to take the luggage.

name: BP Chiang Mai City Hotel Old Town
detail: We were pleasantly surprised with our stay here. The hotel is located in the Old City and close to a number of very interesting temples. As others pointed out, the bathrooms could use an upgrade but the hotel is clean, the beds comfortable, a large flat screen television with many English speaking channels. The Wi-Fi worked excellent in our room and everywhere in hotel. And did I mention they have a great little pool for cooling off after a hot day hiking around town? The staff was extremely friendly and helpful. The front desk helped us book a trip to Chiang Rai and everything worked out great. I would highly recommend this hotel to anyone. You will be hard pressed to find a better value for the money. I'd also recommend booking your hotel with the included breakfast. The breakfast has a good selection of both western and Asian items and you could even get omelets or eggs cooked to order. Coffee, tea, juice, toast and a good selection of fresh fruits were also available. Next trip to Chiang Mai I won't need to research a place to stay, I'll be staying here.
area: Old City,Chiang Mai - View on map

name: Arbors House
detail: Thank you so much for the efforts the host tried to give me.

name: Dusit Princess Chiang Mai
detail: i've been coming back to this hotel year after year. i'd have to say this is one of the best hotels in chiang mai, the staff really go out of their way to make you feel at home. the food is great, the service is great. there's even avis in the hotel if u need to rent a car. its location near the night bazaar and restaurants is great coz transportation is not that easy in chiang mai unless if ur renting a car. the taxi drivers and tuk tuk will always over charge you. i recommend renting a car coz its not that expensive plus u get to see more.
area: Night Bazaar,Chiang Mai - View on map

name: Nature Boutique Hotel at Chiangmai
detail: I stayed in a dorm under the roof. The dorm has 2 AC and a washbin. Outside the dorm is the shared toilet and bathroom. The locker for the luggage are to small to put a back pack inside. It's just for your valuables. Everything was very clean. You get a blanket and a pillow but no towel. At the terrace you have a wunderful view over Chiang Mai. Nearby is the somphet market and many temples. It's no problem to find breakfast in the surrounding.
area: Old City,Chiang Mai - View on map

name: Movenpick Suriwongse Chiang Mai
detail: This was a great find for us. We have stayed in Chiang Mai before in a similar location, but the Suriwongse Hotel is great value for money. The accommodation is excellent! We were upgraded to a Suite on the sixth floor which was very well appointed, comfortable and the room service, just great. The location, right in the middle of the night markets and within easy walking distance to the old city makes this hotel a great option for families, couples and groups. The Hotel has a good restaurant, Bistro and bar. Additionally, there is a good Internet Cafe and two tourist desks. We thought that the service was friendly and efficient and all of the staff we met were helpful. The Bonus was the Friday Night Food Fair which for 100 baht per person provided a good opportunity to try North Thailand Cuisine. I was so impressed with the attentiveness of the staff, the quality of the food and value for money. I have advised my friends to stay at the Suriwongse Hotel when next in Chiang Mai, as we will.
area: Night Bazaar,Chiang Mai - View on map

name: hobnob Hostel
detail: It’s really lovely hostel, it’s new one from October of 2018; the hostess is so friendly and helpful, he advised me tour, sight seeing, the most famous place in Chiang Mai and some tip to travel in Chiang Mai. The room is so clean and bath room also. Kitchen area is public area but so tidy. It’s one of hostel where nice decoration I have ever stayed. I will choose Hobnob for next time in Chiang Mai. Thank you, hostess, you are so friendly, thank you for your motorbike which you gave me in midnight, although you had finished working because of I wanted to go to Night Market.
area: Old City,Chiang Mai - View on map

name: De Lanna Hotel
detail: i have stayed at different hotels in chiang mai and they all fulfills different needs. this hotel is in the middle of old town - close to everything. walking distance. it is new which normally, as in this case, means fresch. my room was nice and on ground level-perfect and close to pool. this is my first hotel in chiang mai with good internet. i missed exersice facitlities or at least connection to it.
area: Old City,Chiang Mai - View on map
